title: gh#43: Phase 11 follow-up: calendar conflict tooltip interaction regression test
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/43.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Context

Phase 11 UAT (test 18) confirmed the conflict-indicator path end-to-end:

- Server annotates events with `hasConflict: boolean` (verified by `packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/calendar-api.test.ts`)
- `eventPropGetter` applies `!border-l-destructive` when `event.hasConflict` (verified by `packages/web/src/pages/calendar/__tests__/CalendarPage.test.tsx`)
- The tooltip's text is rendered in `CalendarEventContent` with the exact UI-SPEC line 159 wording: `Another post on this profile is scheduled within 5 minutes of this time: "{textPreview}" at {date}.`

What's missing is a Vitest assertion that **the tooltip actually opens on hover/focus and renders that text in the DOM**. Today this is confirmed only by code inspection.

## Acceptance criteria

- Add to `packages/web/src/pages/calendar/__tests__/CalendarPage.test.tsx`:
  1. Render with at least two events on the same profile within 5 minutes (both `hasConflict: true`)
  2. Hover (or keyboard-focus) one of the conflict events
  3. Assert the tooltip content with the exact UI-SPEC line 159 wording is present in the DOM
- All existing tests in the file still pass.

## Why now

Discovered during Phase 11 UAT (see `.planning/phases/11-snippets-search-calendar-polish/11-UAT.md` test 18 note). The interaction works in production — this is regression insurance, not a defect.

recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#43 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
